regimeSub: June CPI at 3.5% vs 3.8% expected triggers risk-on as Warsh's July hike case weakens materially.
themes: June CPI prints 3.5% vs 3.8% expected → front-end yields likely to compress as July FOMC hike probability fades, SPX pushing 7549
Chair Warsh's hawkish credibility tested: vowed zero tolerance above 2% target on July 3, but cooling inflation buys time before August decision
SK Hynix crashes 15% in Seoul on HBM contract ceiling concerns → KOSPI vulnerable despite won at 1489 as BOK signals rate hike this week per Yonhap
Trump tears up Iran ceasefire July 9, orders Spain trade cutoff → Brent rebounds to $84 as OPEC+ expands August quotas into renewed Middle East risk

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Real rate ~0.71% (3.63 policy vs 3.5% CPI); soft CPI eases pressure on Warsh to hike July

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: Curve at +35bp (4.56-4.21); steepening reflects growth intact with inflation cooling

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Credit calm with VIX 16.48; risk-on bid following CPI beat keeps spreads compressed

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 16.48 subdued despite Iran ceasefire collapse; CPI relief dominates geopolitical noise

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $84.34 up from post-ceasefire lows; Trump Iran reversal + OPEC+ August hike creates two-way risk

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4070 stabilizing after worst monthly drop since 2008 in June; real rate pressure easing

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: KRW 1489 weak; BOK rate hike this week (Yonhap) needed to defend currency as SK Hynix craters

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix -15% Seoul record rout on HBM contract limits; TSMC earnings Thursday will set sector tone

headline: Soft June CPI (3.5% vs 3.8% exp) hands Warsh breathing room as Iran ceasefire collapse and SK Hynix meltdown create crosscurrents beneath the surface relief rally.
